title: Role of Covariance Matrix in Discriminant Analysis
description: This diagram illustrates how the shared covariance matrix is used to calculate the discriminant function for multiple classes, highlighting the assumption of equal variance-covariance structure across classes.
relationships: Data is used to calculate class-specific means
Class means and data are used to calculate a single, pooled covariance matrix
The shared covariance matrix and class means are plugged into the linear discriminant function δ_k(x) = x^T Σ^{-1} μ_k - 1/2 μ_k^T Σ^{-1} μ_k + log(π_k)
The function δ_k(x) is evaluated for a new observation x for each class k
The class with the highest value from its discriminant function is selected as the prediction
